channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Israel strikes building in southern Beirut, killing at least 3 people
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military struck a building in Beirut's southern suburbs early Tuesday, killing at least three people, in an attack it said said it targeted a member of the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Wild weather blamed for deaths of 3 kids in Michigan and a man in an Amish buggy in Indiana
item.description: KALAMAZOO, Mich. (AP) — A tree fell on a van in Michigan, killing three children, during a fierce weekend storm that swept the region and contributed to the death of an 84-year-old man in an Amish buggy in Indiana, authorities said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's nominee to be the next Joint Chiefs chairman will face senators' questions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s pick to be the next chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan “Razin” Caine, will face questions from senators during his confirmation hearing Tuesday about his qualifications to become the top U.S. military officer.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: On the heels of a dry winter, firefighters around the US brace for wildfire risks
item.description: PRESCOTT, Ariz. (AP) — From the southwestern U.S. to Minnesota, Iowa and even parts of New Jersey, it seemed that winter never materialized.
item.dc:creator: TY ONEIL and SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Paul Mescal, Barry Keoghan, Joseph Quinn and Harris Dickinson to star in 4 biopics of The Beatles
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Hollywood studio executives are making their case for theatergoing with a little help from John Lennon,Paul McCartney,George Harrison and Ringo Starr — along with the newly announced stars set to play them.
item.dc:creator: KRYSTA FAURIA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hooters files for bankruptcy, says the chain's down but not out
item.description: Made famous by its serving staff’s uniforms - low-cut tank tops and orange shorts - and, later, its chicken wings, Hooters has filed for bankruptcy, it announced.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KTLA 5

item.title: Evacuations ordered as winds pose challenges for firefighters battling California blaze
item.description: BISHOP, Calif. (AP) — Firefighters stopped the forward spread of a wildfire tearing through a remote area of eastern California, but evacuation orders for residents of hundreds of homes remained in place, officials said Monday evening.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Girl, 9, dies after procedure at California dental office
item.description: A report from the San Diego County Medical Examiner's Office claims the girl was discharged from the facility and remained asleep on the ride home.
item.dc:creator: Delaney White, ABC 4

item.title: Judge postpones Trump Administration effort to rescind deportation protections for Venezuelans
item.description: On Monday, a federal judge in California issued a temporary injunction, postponing a Trump Administration effort to rescind deportation protections.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Rogers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Colorado man federally charged in Tesla firebomb attack: 'Justice is coming,' AG Pam Bondi says
item.description: A Colorado man arrested in Texas for allegedly firebombing a Tesla dealership in Colorado is facing federal charges in addition to state charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nonprofit groups and Democrats sue Trump administration over election executive order
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to overhaul the nation's elections faced its first legal challenges Monday as the Democratic National Committee and a pair of nonprofits filed two separate lawsuits calling it unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CBP in Texas confiscates 242 pounds of smuggled Mexican bologna at port of entry
item.description: A man was stopped for the second time in two months, allegedly trying to smuggle several pounds of Mexican bologna into the U.S. through an El Paso, Texas port.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he expects Musk to eventually return to running companies
item.description: President Trump on Monday said he expects Elon Musk will eventually return to running his companies after his work overhauling the federal government. A reporter noted that Musk, the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, is a special government employee, which means he can only work for 130 days in his role.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of workers at nation's health agencies brace for mass layoffs
item.description: As they readied to leave work Monday, some workers at the Food and Drug Administration were told to pack their laptops and prepare for the possibility that they wouldn’t be back.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz And Matthew Perrone, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Remains found in California decades ago ID'd as missing New York 13-year-old
item.description: A 13-year-old girl who was found dead along a river in California's Santa Cruz County three decades ago was recently identified through DNA testing, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KREX 5

item.title: Georgia lawmakers agree on school safety bill after Apalachee High School shooting
item.description: Georgia lawmakers said repeatedly that a student-tracking database had been removed from a school safety bill.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Photos show living conditions of Connecticut man allegedly held captive for over 20 years
item.description: Dozens of photos obtained by Nexstar's WTNH show the living conditions authorities found in the Waterbury, Connecticut, home where a man says he was held captive by his stepmom, Kimberly Sullivan, for over 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KRON4

item.title: Report: 1 in 5 Catholics in US affected by deportations
item.description: One in five Catholic families in the United States stands to feel the pain from the Trump administration's promised mass deportations, a group of faith leaders said.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Judge blocks release of some records related to deaths of Gene Hackman, Betsy Arakawa
item.description: A New Mexico judge has decided to block the release of public records showing the bodies of Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Cruz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A lawsuit by inmates is challenging Tennessee's plans to resume executions. Here's how
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Nine Tennessee death row inmates are suing the state over its push for a new round of lethal injections after an execution was abruptly called off in 2022 and a follow-up investigation found scores of missteps in several executions.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Odds of a recession are rising, top economists warn
item.description: Goldman Sachs now sees a 35% chance of a U.S. recession in the next 12 months, up from 20% previously.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: 9-year-old girl dies after leaving dental office
item.description: A dental office in Vista released a statement Sunday regarding the recent passing of a 9-year-old girl following a procedure at the facility.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Under the new Trump administration, this year’s Transgender Day of Visibility has a different tenor
item.description: On the campaign trail, Donald Trump used contentiousness around transgender people's access to sports and bathrooms to fire up conservative voters and sway undecideds.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration deports more migrants to Salvadoran prison
item.description: The Trump administration has deported another 17 men it claims are affiliated with gangs to a Salvadoran prison.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iowa food bank facing challenges after USDA cuts
item.description: “The Food Bank of Siouxland will lose about 70,000 pounds of product,” Director Jacob Wanderscheid said. “That represents about $140,000 worth of value to the food bank.
item.dc:creator: Chase Foix, NewsNation

item.title: Comedian Paul Rodriguez arrested on drug charge in California, police say
item.description: Rodriguez, 70, was booked at the Burbank Police Jail for narcotics possession and later released with a citation to appear in court on April 25, Sgt. Turner elaborated.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KRON4

item.title: Wisconsin appeals court won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters after attorney general sues
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin appellate court denied the state Democratic attorney general's request to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters at a rally planned for Sunday, just two days before a closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Betts, top overall seed UCLA look for March Madness success in Elite Eight meeting with LSU
item.description: With back-to-back games of at least 30 points and 10 or more rebounds, UCLA center Lauren Betts certainly appears unstoppable. But, LSU has thwarted the Bruins before — in the Sweet 16 of last years women’s NCAA Tournament. They'll get a rematch on Sunday, this time in the Elite Eight.
item.dc:creator: ANNE M. PETERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, KRON4

item.title: Texas reaches 400 measles cases as US deals with outbreaks in 5 states
item.description: At least five states have active measles outbreaks as of Friday, and Texas' is the largest with 400 cases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Signal chat shows isolationist Vance as Trump flexes control abroad
item.description: The war plans group chat uncovered by a journalist at The Atlantic exposed some daylight between Vice President Vance and others in the administration, showing Vance’s hesitance about launching a military strike that President Trump has hailed this week as necessary and successful.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's election order creates much confusion before the next federal election in 2026
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to change how U.S. elections are run is creating uncertainty for state and local election officials and worries about voter confusion before the next federal election, the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Friday that Denmark has "underinvested" in Greenland’s security and demanded that Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump pushes to take over the Danish territory. The pointed remarks came as Vance visited U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Voice of America wins in court, for now, as judge blocks Trump administration from firing staff
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Voice of America can’t be silenced just yet. A federal judge on Friday halted the Trump administration’s efforts to dismantle the eight-decade-old U.S. government-funded international news service, calling the move a “classic case of arbitrary and capricious decision making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Naval Academy ends affirmative action in admissions
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — The U.S. Naval Academy will no longer consider race, ethnicity or sex as a factor for admission to the service institution, a response to an executive order by President Donald Trump, according to federal court documents made public Friday.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Wall Street tumbles on worries about slower economy, inflation
item.description: Another wipeout is swamping Wall Street Friday on worries about potential worsening inflation and the U.S. economy slowing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FAA acknowledges it must do better after deadly DC air collision
item.description: The head of the Federal Aviation Administration told Congress during a hearing Thursday about a midair collision over Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people that the agency must do more to ensure flying remains safe.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4
